message:
feat(a11y): add accessibility hooks for motion preferences

Add two new React hooks that enable accessible animations throughout
the wizard and flywheel pages by respecting user motion preferences.

New hook: useReducedMotion (apps/web/lib/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts)
- Detects user's `prefers-reduced-motion` media query preference
- Returns boolean indicating if reduced motion is preferred
- Listens for preference changes (e.g., user toggles system setting)
- Includes useAccessibleAnimation() helper that returns animation
  props only when motion is acceptable, empty object otherwise
- Handles SSR gracefully by defaulting to false on server

New hook: useScrollReveal (apps/web/lib/hooks/useScrollReveal.ts)
- Scroll-triggered reveal animations using IntersectionObserver
- Automatically respects reduced motion (shows immediately if set)
- Configurable options:
  - threshold: visibility percentage to trigger (default: 0.1)
  - rootMargin: adjust trigger point (default: "0px 0px -50px 0px")
  - triggerOnce: animate only first time in view (default: true)
  - delay: ms to wait before revealing (default: 0)
  - disabled: skip animation entirely (default: false)
- Returns ref, isInView, and hasBeenInView states
- Includes staggerDelay() utility for cascading list animations
- Includes useScrollRevealMotion() variant with pre-configured
  Framer Motion props (spring animation, y: 24 offset)

These hooks support WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.3.3 (Animation
from Interactions) by providing easy patterns for respecting user
preferences without sacrificing visual polish for users who want it.
